result.markdown:
--- title: "India's Trade Statistics: Exports, Imports & Sectoral Composition" description: "Interactive analysis of India's total merchandise trade — exports, imports, trade balance, and sector-level composition including cereals, fertilizers, and petroleum — using HS code classification." author: "Pawan" date: "2025/10/29" date-modified: last-modified categories: - "Trade" - "Economy" format: html: code-fold: true toc: true toc-depth: 3 number-sections: true theme: flatly editor_options: chunk_output_type: console execute: error: false message: false warning: false eval: true --- ## Overview {.unnumbered} India's merchandise trade has grown from under USD 200 billion (2005) to over USD 1.1 trillion (2023), making it the world's 10th largest merchandise trading nation. This analysis examines **total export and import trends**, the **trade balance trajectory**, and the composition of key sectors — cereals, fertilizers, petroleum, and edible oils — using Harmonised System (HS) code classification. --- ::: {.callout-tip title="Key Findings at a Glance"} - 📈 **India's exports crossed USD 450 billion** in 2022–23, up from ~USD 170 billion a decade earlier - 📉 **Trade deficit is structural and widening**: typically USD 200–250 billion annually in recent years - 🌾 **Rice dominates cereal exports**: India is the world's largest rice exporter, accounting for ~40% of global trade - ⛽ **Petroleum products are India's top export category** — a testament to refinery capacity expansions - 🌱 **Fertilizer imports are large and import-intensive**: India imports ~$10 billion worth of fertilizers annually - 🔄 **Export coverage ratio has declined** from ~70% (early 2000s) to ~55% (recent years), indicating growing import dependence ::: --- ## Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(data.table) library(ggplot2) library(ggiraph) library(readxl) library(viridis) library(scales) my_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) + theme( text = element_text(color = "#2c3e50"), plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(8, 0, 8, 0)), plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, color = "#7f8c8d"), plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "#95a5a6", hjust = 0), axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(size = 10), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank(), panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#ecf0f1", linewidth = 0.5), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#f8f9fa", color = NA), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold") ) std_opts <- list( opts_tooltip( css = paste0("background-color:#2c3e50;color:white;padding:8px 12px;", "border-radius:6px;font-size:12px;line-height:1.5;") ), opts_hover(css = "stroke-width:2.5;opacity:1;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;"), opts_zoom(min = 0.5, max = 3), opts_toolbar(position = "topright"), opts_sizing(rescale = TRUE, width = 1) ) ``` ::: ## Data Loading & Cleaning ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} usd <- as.data.table(readRDS("/home/pawan/trade/totaltrade/total_exportsusd.rds")) qty <- as.data.table(readRDS("/home/pawan/trade/totaltrade/total_exportsqty.rds")) musd <- as.data.table(readRDS("/home/pawan/trade/totaltrade/total_importsusd.rds")) mqty <- as.data.table(readRDS("/home/pawan/trade/totaltrade/total_importsqty.rds")) twodigit <- as.data.table(read_excel("/home/pawan/trade/totaltrade/two-digit.xlsx")) ## Standardised cleaning function clean_trade <- function(dt, val_col) { dt[, `:=`(HSCode2 = substr(HSCode, 1, 2), HSCode4 = substr(HSCode, 1, 4), HSCode6 = substr(HSCode, 1, 6))] dt[, (val_col) := as.numeric(gsub(",", "", get(val_col)))] dt[is.na(get(val_col)), (val_col) := 0] dt } usd <- clean_trade(usd, "Export") qty <- clean_trade(qty, "Export") musd <- clean_trade(musd, "Import") mqty <- clean_trade(mqty, "Import") ## Merge HS classification twodigit[, HSCode2 := as.numeric(HSCode2)] qty[, HSCode2 := as.numeric(HSCode2)] mqty[, HSCode2 := as.numeric(HSCode2)] qty <- merge(qty, twodigit, by = "HSCode2", all.x = TRUE) mqty <- merge(mqty, twodigit, by = "HSCode2", all.x = TRUE) ## Aggregate totals export_usd <- usd[, .(Export_USD = sum(Export, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year] import_usd <- musd[, .(Import_USD = sum(Import, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year] export_qty <- qty[, .(Export_Qty = sum(Export, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year] import_qty <- mqty[, .(Import_Qty = sum(Import, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year] trade <- Reduce(function(a, b) merge(a, b, by = "Year"), list(export_usd, import_usd, export_qty, import_qty)) trade[, `:=`( Trade_Balance = Export_USD - Import_USD, Trade_Volume = Export_USD + Import_USD, Export_Coverage = round(Export_USD * 100 / Import_USD, 1), Export_Growth = round((Export_USD / shift(Export_USD) - 1) * 100, 1), Import_Growth = round((Import_USD / shift(Import_USD) - 1) * 100, 1) )] ``` ::: --- ## Trade Balance Trend ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} trade_long <- melt(trade[, .(Year, Export_USD, Import_USD)], id = "Year", variable.name = "Flow", value.name = "USD") trade_long[, tip := paste0("", ifelse(Flow == "Export_USD", "Exports", "Imports"), "
", "Year: ", Year, "
", "Value: USD ", format(round(USD / 1000), big.mark = ","), " Bn")] pt <- ggplot() + ## Trade balance area geom_area(data = trade, aes(x = Year, y = Trade_Balance / 1000, group = 1), fill = "#e74c3c", alpha = 0.15) + ## Export & import lines geom_line_interactive( data = trade_long, aes(x = Year, y = USD / 1000, color = Flow, tooltip = tip, data_id = paste(Flow, Year), group = Flow), linewidth = 1.3 ) + geom_point_interactive( data = trade_long, aes(x = Year, y = USD / 1000, color = Flow, tooltip = tip, data_id = paste(Flow, Year)), size = 2.5 ) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "#7f8c8d") + scale_color_manual(values = c("Export_USD" = "#27ae60", "Import_USD" = "#e74c3c"), labels = c("Export_USD" = "Exports", "Import_USD" = "Imports")) + scale_y_continuous(labels = label_dollar(suffix = "B", scale = 1)) + labs(title = "India's Total Merchandise Trade", subtitle = "Exports, imports, and trade deficit (USD Billions)", x = "Year", y = "USD Billions", caption = "Source: Ministry of Commerce, India | Analysis: Pawan") + my_theme girafe(ggobj = pt, width_svg = 11, height_svg = 6, options = std_opts) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Export Coverage Ratio ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} trade[, tip_cov := paste0("Year: ", Year, "
", "Export coverage: ", Export_Coverage, "%
", "(Exports ÷ Imports × 100)")] pc <- ggplot(trade[!is.na(Export_Coverage)], aes(x = Year, y = Export_Coverage, tooltip = tip_cov, data_id = Year, group = 1)) + geom_col_interactive( aes(fill = Export_Coverage < 60), width = 0.75, alpha = 0.85 ) + geom_hline(yintercept = 100, linetype = "dashed", color = "#27ae60", linewidth = 0.8) + geom_hline(yintercept = 60, linetype = "dotted", color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 0.8) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "#3498db", "TRUE" = "#e67e22"), guide = "none") + scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) + labs(title = "Export Coverage Ratio", subtitle = "How much of India's imports are covered by export earnings?", x = "Year", y = "Exports as % of Imports", caption = "Green dashed = 100% (balanced trade); Red dotted = 60% threshold") + my_theme girafe(ggobj = pc, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 5, options = std_opts) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Cereal Exports: Composition India is the world's largest rice exporter and a significant wheat exporter in surplus years. The 2022–23 wheat export ban reflects India's policy of managing domestic food price inflation. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} cereals <- qty[HSCode2 == 10] cereals[, Commodity := fcase( HSCode4 == "1006", "Rice", HSCode4 == "1001", "Wheat", HSCode4 == "1005", "Maize", HSCode4 == "1007", "Grain Sorghum", HSCode4 == "1008", "Millet & Buckwheat", default = "Other Cereals" )] cereals_yr <- cereals[, .(Qty = sum(Export, na.rm = TRUE)), by = .(Year, Commodity)] cereals_yr[, Total := sum(Qty), by = Year] cereals_yr[, Share := round(Qty * 100 / Total)] cereals_yr[, Commodity := factor(Commodity, levels = c("Other Cereals", "Millet & Buckwheat", "Grain Sorghum", "Maize", "Wheat", "Rice"))] cereals_yr[, tip := paste0("", Commodity, "
", "Year: ", Year, "
", "Qty: ", format(round(Qty), big.mark = ","), " '000T
", "Share: ", Share, "%")] pce <- ggplot(cereals_yr, aes(x = Year, y = Qty, fill = Commodity, tooltip = tip, data_id = paste(Commodity, Year))) + geom_bar_interactive(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.75, alpha = 0.9) + geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Share >= 8, paste0(Share, "%"), "")), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 2.8, color = "white", fontface = "bold") + scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "D") + scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format()) + labs(title = "Cereal Exports from India by Commodity", subtitle = "Rice dominates; wheat exports vary by policy and domestic supply conditions", x = "Year", y = "Quantity ('000 Tonnes)", caption = "Source: Ministry of Commerce | Analysis: Pawan") + my_theme girafe(ggobj = pce, width_svg = 11, height_svg = 6, options = std_opts) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Fertilizer Imports: Composition ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} fert <- mqty[HSCode2 == 31] fert[, Commodity := fcase( HSCode4 == "3102", "Nitrogenous (Urea & Others)", HSCode4 == "3104", "Potassic (MOP)", HSCode4 == "3105", "DAP & NPK Compounds", default = "Other Fertilizers" )] fert_yr <- fert[, .(Qty = sum(Import, na.rm = TRUE)), by = .(Year, Commodity)] fert_yr[, Total := sum(Qty), by = Year] fert_yr[, Share := round(Qty * 100 / Total)] fert_yr[, tip := paste0("", Commodity, "
", "Year: ", Year, "
", "Qty: ", format(round(Qty), big.mark = ","), " '000T
", "Share: ", Share, "%")] pfe <- ggplot(fert_yr, aes(x = Year, y = Qty, fill = Commodity, tooltip = tip, data_id = paste(Commodity, Year))) + geom_bar_interactive(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.75, alpha = 0.9) + geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Share >= 8, paste0(Share, "%"), "")), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 2.8, color = "white", fontface = "bold") + scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, option = "C") + scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format()) + labs(title = "Fertilizer Imports by Type", subtitle = "Nitrogenous (urea-based) fertilizers dominate imports by volume", x = "Year", y = "Quantity ('000 Tonnes)", caption = "Source: Ministry of Commerce | Analysis: Pawan") + my_theme girafe(ggobj = pfe, width_svg = 11, height_svg = 6, options = std_opts) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Petroleum Imports: Composition ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} petro <- mqty[HSCode2 == 27] petro[, Commodity := fcase( HSCode4 == "2701", "Coal", HSCode4 == "2709", "Crude Petroleum Oils", HSCode4 == "2711", "Petroleum Gases (LNG/LPG)", default = "Other Mineral Fuels" )] petro_yr <- petro[, .(Qty = sum(Import, na.rm = TRUE)), by = .(Year, Commodity)] petro_yr[, Total := sum(Qty), by = Year] petro_yr[, Share := round(Qty * 100 / Total)] petro_yr[, tip := paste0("", Commodity, "
", "Year: ", Year, "
", "Qty: ", format(round(Qty), big.mark = ","), " '000T
", "Share: ", Share, "%")] ppe <- ggplot(petro_yr, aes(x = Year, y = Qty, fill = Commodity, tooltip = tip, data_id = paste(Commodity, Year))) + geom_bar_interactive(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.75, alpha = 0.9) + geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Share >= 8, paste0(Share, "%"), "")), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 2.8, color = "white", fontface = "bold") + scale_fill_manual(values = c("Crude Petroleum Oils" = "#e67e22", "Coal" = "#2c3e50", "Petroleum Gases (LNG/LPG)" = "#8e44ad", "Other Mineral Fuels" = "#bdc3c7")) + scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format()) + labs(title = "Petroleum & Mineral Fuel Imports by Type", subtitle = "Crude petroleum dominates; LNG imports rising as gas infrastructure expands", x = "Year", y = "Quantity ('000 Tonnes)", caption = "Source: Ministry of Commerce | Analysis: Pawan") + my_theme girafe(ggobj = ppe, width_svg = 11, height_svg = 6, options = std_opts) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Edible Oils & Fats Import Volume ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} oils <- mqty[HSCode2 == 15] oils_yr <- oils[, .(Qty = sum(Import, na.rm = TRUE)), by = Year] oils_yr[, tip := paste0("Year: ", Year, "
", "Edible oils import: ", format(round(Qty / 1000), big.mark = ","), " '000 Tonnes")] po <- ggplot(oils_yr, aes(x = Year, y = Qty / 1000, tooltip = tip, data_id = Year, group = 1)) + geom_area_interactive(fill = "#f39c12", alpha = 0.3) + geom_line_interactive(linewidth = 1.2, color = "#d68910") + geom_point_interactive(size = 2.5, color = "#b7770d") + scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format(suffix = " '000T")) + labs(title = "Vegetable Fats & Edible Oils Import Volume", subtitle = "Annual import trend — HS Chapter 15", x = "Year", y = "Quantity ('000 Tonnes)", caption = "Source: Ministry of Commerce | Analysis: Pawan") + my_theme girafe(ggobj = po, width_svg = 10, height_svg = 5, options = std_opts) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: --- ## Key Takeaways ::: {.callout-important title="Summary of Findings"} 1. **India's trade deficit is deep and structural**: Petroleum, fertilizers, and edible oils together account for the bulk of the import bill, and all three are heavily import-dependent by structural necessity. 2. **Cereal exports are policy-sensitive**: The government alternates between encouraging exports (when there is surplus) and banning them (when domestic prices rise) — illustrating the tension between farmer welfare and consumer welfare. 3. **India is the world's largest rice exporter**: Rice contributes the bulk of cereal export earnings, though the recent quality and residue concerns in some markets require attention. 4. **Petroleum imports are the largest single category**: India's energy import dependence (~85% crude oil from overseas) is both an economic vulnerability and a geopolitical consideration. 5. **The export coverage ratio declining** toward 55% suggests imports are growing faster than exports — a medium-term current account concern. 6. **Fertilizer import dependence** reinforces the need to expand domestic production capacity and reduce over-subsidisation, which incentivises excessive fertilizer use and soil degradation. :::
